Portfolio

Jason is a Unity Developer and Instructional Designer living in Charlottesville, VA. Get In Touch

Inside a Particle Accelerator VR Visualizer

A VR learning experience developed for Oculus Quest about how physicists discover the interior of a proton structure using an electron accelerator.

Inside a Particle Accelerator allows learners to "follow an electron" as it travels inside a particle accelerator. They watch the reaction step-by-step as the electron causes the proton target to emit photons that are detected, slowly forming a picture of the proton's structure. View Case Study

Flowerdew Hundred Sim

A visualization of the Harris Matrix for students learning about archeology.

The Flowerdew Hundred Unity Sim is a WebGL widget that connects to a Drupal Database, allowing students to explore real data from an archeological dig and visualize it in relation to models and dirt strata. View Case Study

Papercraft DC Motor

A project-based exploration of electromagnetism for high school students using physical and virtual prototypes.

The Papercraft DC Motor is a learning module for high schools that combines die-cutting, electronics, AR, and VR to explore electromagnetism. View Case Study

Hey, I’m Jason, nice to meet you!

I’m an Interactive & Immersive Developer as well as an Instructional Designer.

I specialize in (nearly) full-stack creation of interactive education and training games, simulations, and experiences. I have a masters in Instructional Design (M.Ed. University of Virginia 2016) and a long professional history of UX Design, so I work to ensure that products are optimized for minds as well as devices. I develop in Unity and model in Blender.

I'd love to hear from you and start a conversation about your project.
